pattern: A substring where every present letter shares one frequency forces an O(n^2) window check unless you reason about how the count vector evolves as the window grows.
hints: A length-1000 string lets you afford checking every starting position and extending one character at a time.
While extending a window, keep a running tally of how many times each letter has appeared; the window is balanced when all the nonzero tallies are equal.

For each left endpoint, walk right maintaining 26 counts and the current distinct-letter count; whenever max count times distinct equals the window length, the window is balanced and you can update the best length.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct letters each appearing twice as a block
balanced region is interior, surrounded by unbalancing letters
palindromic pair pattern that is balanced as a whole
only length-2 windows balance, never longer

stress.description: small alphabet random string so balanced windows occur frequently and the naive scan stays exact
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
